title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. urrielense
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. urrielense, a subspecies of the rockrose plant, is a flowering perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Balearic Islands. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. urrielense include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
